channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Elon Musk’s China ties make a bad situation worse
item.description: In the first weeks of Donald Trump’s second term in office, Elon Musk has been busy seizing control of instruments of government power, causing many to wonder who is really in charge. “Elon Musk is President,” The Atlantic’s Jonathan Lemire declared. “There has never been a private citizen like him.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: LinkedIn is releasing even more video tools as it looks to take on TikTok, Instagram
item.description: LinkedIn is making it easier for users to engage with videos, and easier for creators to see the results.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: LinkedIn’s bet on TikTok-style video is paying off in a big way
item.description: Is LinkedIn the new TikTok? Short-form video is now the fastest-growing category on LinkedIn, growing at twice the rate of other post formats on the platform. According to LinkedIn, total video viewership surged 36% in the first quarter of 2025.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Israeli spyware company confirms US government and friends are customers
item.description: Israeli spyware company Paragon says the US government is a customer, shortly after it was called out by WhatsApp for targeting journalists.
item.dc:creator: benedict.collins@futurenet.com (Benedict Collins), TechRadar

item.title: If this Xiaomi 15 Ultra camera rumor is true, it could rival the Galaxy S25 Ultra
item.description: A new rumor suggests the Xiaomi 15 Ultra will come equipped with one of the most powerful camera systems ever fitted to a smartphone, which could give some of our favorite camera phones a serious run for their money.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Apple Chides Hot Tub Porn App Amid EU Regulatory Shifts
item.description: European Union's Digital Markets Act forces tech giants like Apple to open their services up to more competition, including allowing third-party app store downloads.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Así es el piloto que compara mediante IA generativa ofertas de proveedores en licitaciones
item.description: Carreras Grupo Logístico e IBM, en colaboración con Seidor, han desarrollado un proyecto piloto para comparar automáticamente ofertas de proveedores en licitaciones mediante el uso de inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI-Powered Policing: The Future of Traffic Safety in Kazakhstan
item.description: Traffic management is a growing challenge for cities worldwide, requiring a balance between enforcement, efficiency, and public trust. In Kazakhstan, the Qorgau system is redefining road safety through an innovative fusion of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and mobile technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Protect Your Online Identity: Expert Tips on Hacking Prevention
item.description: Discover how to safeguard your social media accounts from hacking and data-stealing thieves with expert advice from Kim Komando.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: Google’s digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn’t enough to offset investors’ worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be as lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success
item.description: Louis Koplin, the acting program executive officer for the Navy’s PEO-Digital, said priorities for 2025 include cyber resiliency and expanding BYOD. The post Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Hawley makes push to allow parents to sue tech companies
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) said Tuesday he will introduce legislation to empower parents to sue leading social media companies over alleged harms to children and teens.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How an ancient asteroid strike carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the far side.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok’s teen users ponder what’s next for the app
item.description: During TikTok’s 75-day reprieve, dedicated young users weigh alternate sites — or quitting social media.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Urias, The Mercury News

item.title: Adobe enhances Acrobat AI with contract intelligence to streamline enterprise workflows
item.description: Adobe has introduced contract intelligence features in Acrobat AI Assistant, targeting enterprises and professionals dealing with complex agreements.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CIOs grapple with subpar global genAI models
item.description: With the number of generative AI trials soaring in the enterprise, it is typical for the CIO to purchase numerous large language models from various model makers, tweaked for different geographies and languages.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Anthropic unveils new framework to block harmful content from AI models
item.description: Anthropic has showcased a new security framework, designed to reduce the risk of harmful content generated by its large language models (LLM), a move that could have far-reaching implications for enterprise tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI reveals new AI tool that can do online research for you
item.description: OpenAI is further proving that 2025 is the year of agentic AI. The artificial intelligence giant revealed another new feature from ChatGPT this week, called Deep Research, which it claims can gather research from across the web and summarize it in easy-to-read reports.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeepSeek
item.description: DeepSeek, founded in 2023 by Liang Wenfeng, a Chinese entrepreneur, engineer and former hedge fund manager, is generating a lot of buzz — and for good reason. Here are five things that make it stand out (as well as a listing of the latest news and analysis about DeepSeek).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Taiwan chip tariff would raise industry costs, analysts say
item.description: President Donald Trump continues to use tariffs like a club to get his way, and is threatening to place tariffs on computer chips made in Taiwan, warning the additional charges could reach as high as 100%.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft creates a new department to review how AI affects society
item.description: As part of Microsoft’s big push into AI services, the company has created a new department called the Advanced Planning Unit (APU). Employees of the APU will, among other things, study the effects of AI on society and make recommendations about which products are worth investing resources in.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why second-user smartphone sales are good for Apple
item.description: Apple’s iPhone business isn’t just about new product sales. The second-user and services markets need to be considered, too.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most talked-about Super Bowl ads
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says Super Bowl 2025 ads promise star power and laughs, featuring Beckham's twin reveal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Big rigs deliver cargo with no humans at the wheel
item.description: First driverless semi delivers 100 loads in Texas, transforming logistics with AI. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives his takeaways.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The DeepSeek lesson
item.description: During the past two weeks, DeepSeek unraveled Silicon Valley’s comfortable narrative about generative artificial intelligence by introducing dramatically more efficient ways to scale large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VMware offers fixes to severe vulnerabilities in VMware Aria
item.description: VMware has fixed multiple high-severity vulnerabilities affecting its cloud management platform (CMP), VMware Aria, which could allow attackers to steal sensitive credentials from the virtualization giant’s IT management and logging solutions.
item.dc:creator: csoonline
